summary: Iteration 2 full-stack testing of Avero. Backend: 10/10 pytest passing (root health, enquiries CRUD w/ validation, admin JWT login+enquiries+stats+PATCH, chat SSE session/delta/done). Confirmed chat now responds correctly to 'Do you offer AI marketing?' with 'AI marketing services are coming soon at Avero' — new positioning is honored in system prompt. Frontend: all new sections render on desktop (1920) and mobile (390) — MemeSection (compare stage + slider handle + 3 presets + 3 story beats + 2 CTAs), Laptop3D (scene/screen/tabs/prev/next, all 3 tab contents cycle), single mega-offer Pricing card (₹15,000 struck-through + ₹3,999 + 14 features + 2 CTAs), Roadmap (4 items + CTA). Hero subheadline updated to 'Avero is a modern IT studio'. Portfolio cards render (mshots URLs load, no image crashes).

ui_bugs.issue: HTML nesting warning: <button data-testid='laptop3d-screen'> wraps EcommerceScreen which contains an <button> ('Add' button in product cards). Causes React hydration warning: 'In HTML, <button> cannot be a descendant of <button>'. Non-blocking (UI still works) but violates HTML spec.

action_items: (Optional/low priority) Fix nested <button> in Laptop3D: change the inner 'Add' button inside EcommerceScreen to a <span> or <div role='button'> (or make the outer laptop3d-screen a <div role='button' onClick=...>) to eliminate the HTML nesting warning.
critical_code_review_comments: Portfolio.jsx uses mshots URLs which may return an empty/placeholder image on first request; the gradient fallback works — leave as-is per user note.
server.py chat model successfully switched to 'openai/gpt-4o-mini' — no more transient 'Budget exceeded' errors observed this iteration.
seed_data_creation: 1 TEST_ enquiry created via test_create_enquiry_full_payload (patched to status=contacted).
context_for_next_testing_agent: All new iteration-2 sections (MemeSection, Laptop3D, Roadmap, mega-offer Pricing) verified on desktop + mobile. Backend chat now uses openai/gpt-4o-mini — stable. Only outstanding item is a low-priority nested-<button> hydration warning in Laptop3D.
